diseases.name: Myocardial Infarction
diseases.description: Commonly known as a heart attack, it occurs when blood flow decreases or stops to a part of the heart, causing damage to the heart muscle.

diseases.name: Pneumonia
diseases.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.

diseases.name: Diabetes Mellitus Type 2
diseases.description: A long-term metabolic disorder that is characterized by high blood sugar, insulin resistance, and relative lack of insulin.

diseases.name: Hypertension
diseases.description: A long-term medical condition in which the blood pressure in the arteries is persistently elevated.

diseases.name: Alzheimer's Disease
diseases.description: A progressive neurologic disorder that causes the brain to shrink and brain cells to die, leading to memory loss and cognitive decline.

diseases.name: Multiple Sclerosis
diseases.description: A potentially disabling disease of the brain and spinal cord where the immune system attacks the protective sheath (myelin) that covers nerve fibers.
prevention: Vitamin D may play a role

diseases.name: Crohn's Disease
diseases.description: A type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) that causes inflammation of your digestive tract, which can lead to abdominal pain and severe diarrhea.

diseases.name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
diseases.description: An autoimmune disease in which the immune system attacks its own tissues, causing widespread inflammation and tissue damage in the affected organs.

diseases.name: Huntington's Disease
diseases.description: A rare, inherited disease that causes the progressive breakdown (degeneration) of nerve cells in the brain.
causes: Autosomal dominant mutation in the HTT gene

diseases.name: Wilson's Disease
diseases.description: A rare genetic disorder that causes copper to accumulate in your liver, brain, and other vital organs.
causes: Mutation in the ATP7B gene (autosomal recessive)
prevention: Genetic screening for siblings of affected individuals

diseases.name: Pheochromocytoma
diseases.description: A rare, usually noncancerous (benign) tumor that develops in an adrenal gland.
causes: Tumor of chromaffin cells in the adrenal medulla

diseases.name: Guillain-Barré Syndrome
diseases.description: A rare disorder in which your body's immune system attacks your nerves, often preceded by an infectious illness.

diseases.name: Cystic Fibrosis
diseases.description: An inherited life-threatening disorder that damages the lungs and digestive system.
causes: Mutation in the CFTR gene (autosomal recessive)

diseases.name: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS)
diseases.description: A progressive nervous system disease that affects nerve cells in the brain and spinal cord, causing loss of muscle control.

diseases.name: Kawasaki Disease
diseases.description: A condition that causes inflammation in the walls of some blood vessels in the body, primarily affecting children.
causes: Likely an abnormal immune response to an infection

diseases.name: Asthma
diseases.description: A condition in which your airways narrow and swell and may produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.

diseases.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
diseases.description: A chronic inflammatory disorder that can affect more than just your joints, including skin, eyes, lungs, and heart.

diseases.name: Parkinson's Disease
diseases.description: A progressive disorder that affects the nervous system and the parts of the body controlled by the nerves.

diseases.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
diseases.description: Also called chronic kidney failure, involves a gradual loss of kidney function over time.

diseases.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
diseases.description: Occurs when stomach acid frequently flows back into the tube connecting your mouth and stomach (esophagus).

drugs.name: Atorvastatin
drugs.use: Used to lower cholesterol and reduce the risk of heart disease.

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.use: First-line medication for the treatment of type 2 diabetes.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin
drugs.use: Treatment of various bacterial infections such as pneumonia and strep throat.

drugs.name: Lisinopril
drugs.use: Treatment of high blood pressure and heart failure.

drugs.name: Amlodipine
drugs.use: Treatment of high blood pressure and chest pain (angina).

drugs.name: Levothyroxine
drugs.use: Treatment of hypothyroidism.
drugs.dosage: 25-200 mcg once daily on empty stomach

drugs.name: Albuterol
drugs.use: Relief of bronchospasm in asthma and COPD.

drugs.name: Omeprazole
drugs.use: Treatment of GERD and stomach ulcers.

drugs.name: Losartan
drugs.use: Treatment of hypertension and diabetic nephropathy.

drugs.name: Gabapentin
drugs.use: Treatment of nerve pain and seizures.

drugs.name: Sertraline
drugs.use: Treatment of depression, OCD, and panic disorder.

drugs.name: Furosemide
drugs.use: Treatment of edema and hypertension.

drugs.name: Warfarin
drugs.use: Prevention of blood clots.

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.use: Relief of pain and inflammation.

drugs.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
drugs.use: Treatment of hypertension and edema.

drugs.name: Prednisone
drugs.use: Treatment of inflammatory and autoimmune conditions.

drugs.name: Clopidogrel
drugs.use: Prevention of stroke and heart attack.

drugs.name: Metoprolol
drugs.use: Treatment of high blood pressure, chest pain, and heart failure.

drugs.name: Azithromycin
drugs.use: Treatment of various bacterial infections.
drugs.dosage: 500 mg on day 1, then 250 mg daily for 4 days

drugs.name: Simvastatin
drugs.use: Used to lower cholesterol and triglycerides.
drugs.dosage: 10-40 mg once daily in the evening

drugs.name: Pantoprazole
drugs.use: Treatment of erosive esophagitis and GERD.

drugs.name: Escitalopram
drugs.use: Treatment of depression and generalized anxiety disorder.

drugs.name: Montelukast
drugs.use: Prevention and chronic treatment of asthma.
drugs.dosage: 10 mg once daily in the evening

drugs.name: Rosuvastatin
drugs.use: Used to lower cholesterol and reduce the risk of stroke and heart attack.

drugs.name: Bupropion
drugs.use: Treatment of major depressive disorder and smoking cessation.

drugs.name: Duloxetine
drugs.use: Treatment of depression, anxiety, and diabetic peripheral neuropathy.

drugs.name: Venlafaxine
drugs.use: Treatment of depression, anxiety, and panic disorder.

drugs.name: Ranitidine
drugs.use: Treatment of ulcers and GERD.

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
drugs.use: Treatment of various bacterial infections.

drugs.name: Cephalexin
drugs.use: Treatment of skin, respiratory, and urinary tract infections.

drugs.name: Doxycycline
drugs.use: Treatment of various bacterial infections and acne.

drugs.name: Fluconazole
drugs.use: Treatment of fungal infections.
drugs.dosage: 150 mg single dose for vaginal candidiasis

drugs.name: Ondansetron
drugs.use: Prevention of nausea and vomiting caused by chemotherapy or surgery.

drugs.name: Spironolactone
drugs.use: Treatment of heart failure, hypertension, and edema.

drugs.name: Digoxin
drugs.use: Treatment of heart failure and atrial fibrillation.

drugs.name: Insulin Glargine
drugs.use: Long-acting insulin used to improve blood sugar control in adults and children with diabetes mellitus.
drugs.dosage: Individualized; usually once daily at the same time

drugs.name: Amoxicillin/Clavulanate
drugs.use: Treatment of infections caused by bacteria, such as sinusitis, pneumonia, and ear infections.

drugs.name: Diazepam
drugs.use: Treatment of anxiety, muscle spasms, and alcohol withdrawal.

drugs.name: Fluoxetine
drugs.use: Treatment of major depressive disorder, OCD, and bulimia nervosa.

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.use: Relief of mild to moderate pain and fever.
sideEffects: Rare at therapeutic doses; liver damage in overdose

caseStudies.title: Acute Chest Pain in a 55-year-old Male
caseStudies.history: A 55-year-old male presents to the ER with sudden onset substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm.
caseStudies.symptoms: Crushing chest pain, diaphoresis, nausea.
caseStudies.labResults: Elevated Troponin I, ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF.
caseStudies.explanation: The clinical presentation and ECG findings are classic for an inferior wall MI, likely due to occlusion of the Right Coronary Artery (RCA).

caseStudies.title: Progressive Shortness of Breath
caseStudies.history: A 68-year-old female with a history of smoking presents with worsening dyspnea over the past 3 months.
caseStudies.symptoms: Productive cough, wheezing, barrel chest, use of accessory muscles.
caseStudies.labResults: Reduced FEV1/FVC ratio on spirometry, hyperinflated lungs on X-ray.
caseStudies.explanation: The patient's smoking history combined with obstructive patterns on spirometry and physical findings strongly suggest COPD.

caseStudies.title: Sudden Weakness and Slurred Speech
caseStudies.history: A 72-year-old male is brought to the hospital after his wife noticed he couldn't move his right side and was speaking incoherently.
caseStudies.symptoms: Right-sided hemiplegia, facial drooping, aphasia.
caseStudies.labResults: CT scan shows no hemorrhage; MRI reveals ischemic area in the left middle cerebral artery territory.
caseStudies.explanation: Sudden focal neurological deficits in a patient with cardiovascular risk factors are highly suggestive of a stroke. Imaging confirmed an ischemic event.

caseStudies.title: Abdominal Pain and Fever
caseStudies.history: A 24-year-old female presents with periumbilical pain that has shifted to the right lower quadrant.
caseStudies.symptoms: Rebound tenderness at McBurney's point, fever, anorexia.
caseStudies.labResults: Elevated WBC count (leukocytosis), ultrasound shows inflamed appendix.
caseStudies.explanation: The migration of pain and localized tenderness in the RLQ are classic signs of appendicitis, confirmed by leukocytosis and imaging.

caseStudies.title: Excessive Thirst and Fatigue
caseStudies.history: A 10-year-old boy is brought in by his parents due to extreme thirst and frequent nighttime urination.
caseStudies.symptoms: Polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss despite increased appetite.
caseStudies.labResults: Random blood glucose of 350 mg/dL, ketones present in urine.
caseStudies.explanation: The classic triad of polyuria, polydipsia, and weight loss in a child with hyperglycemia and ketonuria indicates Type 1 Diabetes.

caseStudies.title: Joint Pain and Morning Stiffness
caseStudies.history: A 45-year-old female presents with symmetrical joint pain in her hands and feet, particularly worse in the morning for over an hour.
caseStudies.symptoms: Swollen, tender joints, fatigue, morning stiffness.
caseStudies.labResults: Positive Rheumatoid Factor (RF) and anti-CCP antibodies, elevated ESR.
caseStudies.explanation: Symmetrical small joint involvement with prolonged morning stiffness and positive serology is highly indicative of RA.

caseStudies.title: Sudden Onset of Severe Headache
caseStudies.history: A 50-year-old female describes the 'worst headache of her life' that started suddenly while she was lifting a heavy object.
caseStudies.symptoms: Severe headache, neck stiffness, photophobia, vomiting.
caseStudies.labResults: CT head shows blood in the subarachnoid space; lumbar puncture shows xanthochromia.
caseStudies.explanation: A 'thunderclap' headache with meningeal signs is a medical emergency, often due to a ruptured cerebral aneurysm.

caseStudies.title: Chronic Diarrhea and Weight Loss
caseStudies.history: A 32-year-old male reports chronic diarrhea, bloating, and unintentional weight loss over the past 6 months.
caseStudies.symptoms: Steatorrhea, abdominal distension, fatigue.
caseStudies.labResults: Positive anti-tissue transglutaminase (tTG) IgA, biopsy shows villous atrophy.
caseStudies.explanation: Malabsorption symptoms combined with positive serology and characteristic biopsy findings confirm Celiac disease.

caseStudies.title: Palpitations and Heat Intolerance
caseStudies.history: A 28-year-old female presents with racing heart, tremors, and feeling hot even in cool environments.
caseStudies.symptoms: Weight loss despite increased appetite, anxiety, fine tremor, exophthalmos.
caseStudies.labResults: Low TSH, high Free T4, positive TSH receptor antibodies.
caseStudies.explanation: Symptoms of hypermetabolism and specific eye signs (exophthalmos) with suppressed TSH point to Graves' disease.

caseStudies.title: Recurrent Wheezing in a Child
caseStudies.history: A 7-year-old girl has episodes of wheezing and coughing, especially at night and during physical activity.
caseStudies.symptoms: Wheezing, chest tightness, nocturnal cough.
caseStudies.labResults: Reversible airflow obstruction on spirometry after bronchodilator use.
caseStudies.explanation: Recurrent respiratory symptoms with demonstrated reversibility of airflow obstruction is diagnostic of asthma.

caseStudies.title: Sudden Onset of Lower Back Pain
caseStudies.history: A 40-year-old construction worker presents with acute, sharp lower back pain after lifting a heavy beam.
caseStudies.symptoms: Sharp pain radiating down the left leg, numbness in the foot.
caseStudies.labResults: MRI shows herniated disc at L4-L5 level compressing the nerve root.
caseStudies.explanation: Acute onset of radicular pain following heavy lifting is highly suggestive of disc herniation.

caseStudies.title: Persistent Fever and Night Sweats
caseStudies.history: A 35-year-old male reports a low-grade fever, night sweats, and a persistent cough for the past 4 weeks.
caseStudies.symptoms: Cough with occasional blood, weight loss, fatigue.
caseStudies.labResults: Sputum smear positive for acid-fast bacilli, chest X-ray shows apical cavitary lesion.
caseStudies.explanation: Constitutional symptoms with characteristic X-ray findings and positive sputum smear confirm TB.

caseStudies.title: Acute Confusion in an Elderly Patient
caseStudies.history: An 82-year-old female is brought in by her family due to sudden confusion and disorientation over the last 24 hours.
caseStudies.symptoms: Disorientation, urinary frequency, low-grade fever.
caseStudies.labResults: Urinalysis shows positive nitrites and leukocyte esterase, elevated WBC in urine.
caseStudies.explanation: In elderly patients, UTIs often present with acute cognitive changes rather than typical urinary symptoms.

caseStudies.title: Severe Epigastric Pain
caseStudies.history: A 48-year-old male with a history of alcohol use presents with severe, constant epigastric pain radiating to the back.
caseStudies.symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal tenderness, leaning forward for relief.
caseStudies.labResults: Serum amylase and lipase levels are 4 times the upper limit of normal.
caseStudies.explanation: Classic pain pattern with significantly elevated pancreatic enzymes is diagnostic of acute pancreatitis.

caseStudies.title: Sudden Loss of Vision in One Eye
caseStudies.history: A 65-year-old male with hypertension reports sudden, painless loss of vision in his right eye, described as a 'curtain coming down'.
caseStudies.symptoms: Painless vision loss, flashes of light, floaters.
caseStudies.labResults: Funduscopic exam shows grey, opaque retina with folds.
caseStudies.explanation: Sudden painless vision loss with 'curtain' sensation and characteristic funduscopic findings is typical for retinal detachment.

caseStudies.name: Chronic Joint Pain and Stiffness
caseStudies.title: Chronic Joint Pain in a 45-year-old Female
caseStudies.history: A 45-year-old female presents with symmetrical joint pain in her hands and feet, worse in the morning.
caseStudies.symptoms: Swollen joints, morning stiffness lasting >1 hour, fatigue.
caseStudies.labResults: Positive RF and anti-CCP, elevated ESR.
caseStudies.explanation: Symmetrical small joint involvement with prolonged morning stiffness and positive serology is classic for RA.

caseStudies.name: Tremors and Slow Movement
caseStudies.title: Tremors and Movement Difficulty in a 62-year-old Male
caseStudies.history: A 62-year-old male reports a resting tremor in his right hand and difficulty getting out of chairs.
caseStudies.symptoms: Resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigid muscles, masked facies.
caseStudies.labResults: Clinical diagnosis based on physical exam; DaTscan shows reduced dopamine transporters.
caseStudies.explanation: The combination of resting tremor, bradykinesia, and rigidity is the hallmark of Parkinson's disease.

caseStudies.name: Severe Unilateral Headache
caseStudies.title: Recurrent Severe Headache in a 28-year-old Female
caseStudies.history: A 28-year-old female presents with recurrent episodes of throbbing headache on the left side, preceded by visual disturbances.
caseStudies.symptoms: Unilateral throbbing pain, photophobia, phonophobia, visual aura.
caseStudies.labResults: Normal neurological exam between episodes; clinical diagnosis.
caseStudies.explanation: Recurrent unilateral throbbing headaches with associated sensory sensitivities and aura are diagnostic of migraine.

caseStudies.name: Fatigue and Swelling
caseStudies.title: Fatigue and Peripheral Edema in a 55-year-old Male
caseStudies.history: A 55-year-old male with long-standing hypertension reports progressive fatigue and swelling in his ankles.
caseStudies.symptoms: Fatigue, ankle edema, nausea, metallic taste in mouth.
caseStudies.labResults: eGFR of 25 mL/min/1.73m², elevated creatinine, proteinuria.
caseStudies.explanation: Reduced eGFR and proteinuria in the context of hypertension indicate advanced chronic kidney disease.

caseStudies.name: Heartburn and Regurgitation
caseStudies.title: Chronic Heartburn in a 40-year-old Male
caseStudies.history: A 40-year-old male reports frequent heartburn and a sour taste in his mouth, especially after large meals and at night.
caseStudies.symptoms: Heartburn, regurgitation, dry cough, sore throat.
caseStudies.labResults: Endoscopy shows signs of esophagitis.
caseStudies.explanation: Classic symptoms of reflux confirmed by endoscopic evidence of esophageal inflammation.

guidelines.title: Fever Management Protocol
guidelines.description: Steps to evaluate and manage a patient with fever
protocol: Check temperature using a reliable thermometer
Check for associated symptoms (cough, rash, neck stiffness)
Order blood tests (CBC, CRP) or cultures if infection is suspected
Start antipyretics (e.g., Paracetamol) and treat the underlying cause
guidelines.whenToUse: When a patient presents with a body temperature above 38°C (100.4°F).
guidelines.notes: Always rule out red flags like meningitis or sepsis in pediatric and elderly patients.

guidelines.title: Chest Pain Evaluation Protocol
guidelines.description: Systematic approach to evaluating acute chest pain
protocol: Administer Aspirin 325mg if ACS is suspected
Determine if reperfusion therapy is needed
guidelines.whenToUse: Any patient presenting with acute chest discomfort or suspected ACS.
guidelines.notes: Time is muscle. Minimize delays to reperfusion.
